description: Write a TraCI Python script at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/simulate_breakdown.py to dynamically simulate a vehicle breakdown and measure the resulting traffic shockwave in the Bologna Pasubio scenario using the configuration at /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/run.sumocfg. The script must read critical_edge and upstream_edge IDs from /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/target_edges.json. Starting at step 150, identify the first vehicle to appear on the critical_edge, change its color to red, and force it to remain stopped for exactly 300 simulation steps before restoring its normal speed behavior. Throughout the simulation from step 0 to at least step 600, record the number of halted vehicles on both the critical_edge and the upstream_edge. Export this data to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/breakdown_queue.csv with the header step,target_vehicle_id,critical_edge_halted,upstream_edge_halted, using the string NONE for the vehicle ID in steps prior to its identification. Execute the script to generate the CSV file.
